title: Thesis The other impact Neon Genesis Evangelion
display_title: Thesis The other impact Neon Genesis Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: [Thesis] The other impact: Neon Genesis Evangelion...             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Mar 23, 2014 4:22 pm I was browsing what Eva fare there is on Rakuten.com and came across this: The Other Impact: Neon Genesis Evangelion, Postmodernism and Japan in the 1990s. (Paperback) Author: Lauren N. Patton Turns out to be an MA thesis from the University of Wyoming from 2010. Amazon had it for $7.89 at the time of posting but has reverted to $62 . Might be worth a read at the right price. Citation/Abstract Neon Genesis Evangelion is a popular animated television series which ran on Japanese television in the mid-1990s, during the midst of Japan's economic collapse. While much as been written about Evangelion, this study seeks to discuss the series as a text which addresses a postmodern crisis facing Japanese youth during the mid- 1990s due to the economy's troubles. Using a model based on the writings of Frederick Jameson and others, a textual analysis of several episodes which display common characteristics of the postmodern was conducted. The series was found to display all characteristics of the postmodern in ways that reflect the turmoil faced by Japanese youth at the time of the 1990s economic collapse.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Mar 25, 2014 7:50 am Sounds interesting, might even try to see if budget allows me to acquire it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jun 15, 2014 9:40 am I wish I could find out more about this. That's a lot of money to spend when I don't even know if her argument's any good.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jun 15, 2014 11:47 am I have this thesis. I'm not sure how much of it cannot be found somewhere or other on this site, but it does a good job of pulling threads together. Like many theses, it ends by pointing out alternative ways in which the series could be analysed - for instance, what the treatment of Asuka - the only not purely Japanese character - might say about Japan's view of their place in the world, or how the inconclusiveness of the last two episodes mirrors Japan's uncertainty about its own future. (Haven't got time to comment further right now.)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:46 pm Wow the price jumped. I wouldn't pay more than $10 even if she's spot on with her arguments.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:57 pm AR-99 wrote: Wow the price jumped. I wouldn't pay more than $10 even if she's spot on with her arguments.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jun 16, 2014 4:06 pm True, but at $7.89 a few months ago, can't argue with that. Used costing more than new on Amazon, that is just weird.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 16, 2014 4:49 pm AR-99 wrote: Used costing more than new on Amazon, that is just weird.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jun 18, 2014 8:58 pm I have seen that before too. So it looks like I missed the window to purchase this cheap with my Amazon credit. If someone finds it for less, please update.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jun 18, 2014 10:49 pm [s]Hey, I spent about $2 on this book despite the fact that everything about it is utter crap simply because I though it would make an interesting read in the context of Western reaction to its sudden fascination with Eastern media. Maybe $8 isn’t a bad price for this read.[/s] Holy crap! At least $60??? For that price, I’ll just buy a new addition to my Groundworks collection instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 19, 2014 1:40 am I am used to spending exorbitant amounts on academic texts, so whatever. I haven't read through it in its entirety, but it's pretty solid. It's a bit of a pity she focused on 1, 2, 11, 16, 25, and 26, though; I was hoping she'd delve into Anno's use of montage in 22, but that's just my bias showing. Her overall approach is good. And Freaky, this is an actual thesis, not some piece of shit someone cobbled together to score a quick buck. A film guy like you would get a lot out of this.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jun 19, 2014 5:51 am Well, that was a comparison in budgets, not the material. And I won’t have that much disposable income for the next few months anyway, so any Eva purchase will have to wait. Eh, I’ll stick it on a wish list until I actually have that much disposable income. I do want to read it, I can’t be stupid about acquiring it.
